benchmark: Switzerland Gotthard 3km — single-GPU fp64 grid-ceiling characterization
purpose: Honest large-grid speedup attempt; result = fp64 single-GPU grid ceiling, not a speedup number.

cpu_reference.build: dmpar MPI gfortran, 28 ranks (HONEST denominator, NOT 1-core serial)

grid_150x150.note: RRTMG g-point radiation temporary = 25.58 GiB single alloc

grid_128x128.error: CUDA_ERROR_OUT_OF_MEMORY (failed to allocate 21.62MiB at full arena)

conclusion: Single-GPU fp64 grid ceiling on this 32GB RTX 5090 (with ~4GB desktop overhead) is BELOW 128x128. The RRTMG g-point shortwave/longwave radiation temporary dominates VRAM at fp64. Both 150^2 and 128^2 OOM. The production Canary d02 grid fits and runs; the operational speedup is reported on that grid. This is a characterized, honest hardware limit, not a forecast-correctness issue. fp32 would lift the ceiling but detonates the acoustic solver (documented elsewhere); a g-point-batched / chunked RRTMG temp is the v0.13+ lever to raise the fp64 ceiling.
ship_decision: Report operational speedup on the production grid (Canary d02). Document this fp64 grid ceiling in KNOWN_ISSUES / PERFORMANCE. Do NOT chase 112^2 (marginal, not headline-bearing, release clock).
